MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dexter Chan (Petitioner) in Propria Persona.
Peter Bresler appeared by telephone for Sandra Chan Ye (Trustee) and specially appearing for Attorney Michael Freedman for Jennifer Chan.

Mr. Bresler stated that escrow has not closed, that it will close as soon as the clean up construction is completed, that he has provided a list as to what is being done to the units, that the work is being managed by a property management company, that there is no time line for completion/he is not able to provide a hard date and that the management company is proceeding in a reasonable fashion. Mr. Chan stated that he is willing to continue this petition, that part of the agreement is to keep this petition on calendar until close of escrow, that it's the only pressure they have to make sure the Trustee acts and that the contractor's are arguing about payment. The Court noted that this matter has been settled, informed Mr. Chan that if he has concern about settlement, he has to file a new petition, that it would like to see this matter go away and that it will not use this matter to hear what is going on with the settlement. The Petition for Order Suspending Trustee's Powers, etc. is continued to May 30, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 in hopes that they will get escrow closed. The accounting petition in the related matter (PTR-14-298261) on calendar for April 11, 2017 is also continued to May 30, 2017.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Sep-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-14-298179 The Amendment and Restatement of the
Bessie Gin Trust & PTR-14-298261 The Bessie Gin Chan Revocable Living Trust.
Appearances: Emilie Calhoun, moving party. Dexter Chan & April Chan in Propria Persona.

Ms. Calhoun reported that they attended mediation on Monday, that the case has settled, that all parties have signed the global settlement but that she still wants to be relieved as counsel. The Court stated that if the cases are in the last stages it is not inclined to relieve counsel and that it would be appropriate to finish the last step. Ms. Calhoun stated that there is still a lot to be done, that property needs to be sold, that she has not been paid in many months and confirmed that a petition for settlement would be filed within 2 weeks. The Court stated that it does not know why the undisputed portion of the fees cannot be paid and that the clients have a right to arbitrate regarding reasonableness. Ms. Calhoun stated that her burden is to show why she wants to be relieved. The Court stated that counsel also has to convince the Court that it will not effect the clients interests and that it will grant the motion conditioned on counsel remaining through the order for settlement. Mr. Chan stated that they have some objections and concerns and that they are willing to pay to a certain level. The Court stated that it is not fair to ask for representation but to not pay counsel. Ms. Chan stated that they sent a check last month, that they are being perceived as deadbeats and that they need representation through the settlement. The Court stated that it will not require counsel to work without being paid. Mr. Chan stated that they need to know the reasonableness of the fees. The Court stated that it cannot tell them that and that there is a limit as to how far it can protect them. The Court The Court granted the Motions to be relieved as counsel in both matters (subject to receipt/review of the order), instructed Ms. Calhoun to submit a revised form of order conditioned on limitation to getting confirmation on the settlement agreement and told the Chan's that they need to make peace or find a new attorney.

Dexter Chan And April Chans Notice Of Demurrer And Motion To Strike, And Demurrer And Motion To Strike The Ortega Ownership Claim In Trustee Sandra Chan Yees First Amended Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Mar-02-2016 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-02-2016 Continued To Apr-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Parnall for (petitioner & beneficiary) Dexter Chan who is present in court. Edward Koplowitz for (beneficiary) Sandra Yee who is present in court. Peter Bresler for (trustee) Sandra Yee. Michael Freedman for Jennifer Chan.

Mr. Bresler reported that the case is in the process of discovery, that they have not taken depositions yet, that they have been having ongoing discussions, that there is 10 days remaining until the deadline to file a Motion to Compel and that they need a 45 day continuance. Mr. Koplowitz stated that a demurrer has been filed and that a stipulation has been entered into but that they have not seen the order yet. Mr. Parnall stated that the stipulation and order was sent in last week and that it may have gone to Judge Cheng. The Court stated that if the parties have stipulated it does not require the court's signature, that it creates extra work and that the court does not need to work on it. Mr. Parnall stated that the pleadings are hard to follow and that they wanted an order. The Court stated that the court's signature provides nothing and confirmed with counsel that the motion can come off calendar and that whether the order is signed or not there is nothing left. Mr. Parnall stated that since the last time they were here they have gone to mediation, that there were no results, that they believe that the efforts to resolve matters are being undermined and that there is a conflict of interest with the trustee who is also a beneficiary. The Court stated that it is not unusual for a trustee to also be a beneficiary and that she has separate counsel for each capacity. Mr. Parnall stated that the trustee is taking positions that are unreasonable, that she is not abiding by her fiduciary duties, that they are not sure that she is separately represented, that they are asking that she be suspended and that a neutral trustee be appointed, that her positions regarding the property contradict what her father and mother wanted and that the positions she takes are unreasonable. The Court stated that the trustee is entitled to take positions, that it is not grounds for suspension or removal and that objections to the accounting will be dealt with at that time. Mr. Parnall explained their position on the accounting, that they hope to move forward and that they need the playing field leveled. Mr. Koplowitz stated that they have the hearing on the accounting coming up, that setting a status date for 45 days is agreeable, that this case should be settled, that the need a good mediator and that if this matter is tried it will be worse for everyone. Mr. Freedman stated that 45 days is fine, that it will need to be set for trial, that discovery will be more expensive than they had hoped and that in regards to the accounting the case has expended so many resources that the residue may be zero. The Court stated that is does not know what the objections are at this time and that it is not before the court. Mr. Parnall stated that $150,000.00 has been depleted in the last year and asked where it was. The Court stated that if surcharges are appropriate they will come up in due course, that the parties have a choice to avoid the fighting, that it is up to them to settle, that it will not suspend the trustee, that it will not tell the trustee not to defend the trust and that it is sad to see families do this to themselves. The petition for instructions, the petition to set aside and cancel the grant deed, etc., and the petition for order suspending trustee's powers etc., were continued to April 27, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court encourages the parties to figure out a way to settle, to complete discovery, they should have met and conferred regarding the issues and how to litigate and that a joint status statement is to be filed no later than April 20, 2016.

Off Calendar Jul-21-2015 Continued To Nov-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jul-21-2015 Continued To Nov-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz & Peter Bresler for and with (Trustee) Sandra Yee. Emilie Calhoun & David Parnall for and with (Beneficiary) Dexter Chan. Michael Freedman for Jennifer Chan.

Mr. Koplowitz reported that the parties had a mediation session, that a request was made for additional information, that they were to continue on July 13, 2015 but did not, stated that given the family dynamics it makes sense to continue with mediation to try and resolve the issues and that he will file a response to the recent objections. Mr. Parnall reported that Mr. Chan is here for 3 issues;that he wants the trust under court supervision, that he wants the trustee replaced by a professional trustee and that he would like a forensic accounting. Mr. Parnall stated that the 2 primary issues are the ownership of the real property and the status of trust funds, that they are willing to go back to mediation and that the accounting should not be tied to the property. The Court stated that the accounting has been filed in the other case number (PTR-14-298261), that it is not before the court today, that they cannot order an accounting when there already is one and asked why these issues couldn't all be discussed in mediation. Mr. Parnall stated that the real issue with the accounting is that Mr. Chan wants it to go back to 2010, that he has been asking for documents for months and that the documents provided prior to mediation have brought on more unease. Ms. Calhoun stated that it is premature to go to mediation, that the documents were not provided in enough time to review prior to mediation, that they need more discovery on the accounting issue prior to mediation, that they want the powers of the current trustee suspended and the trust under court supervision. Mr. Koplowitz stated that the value of mediation is to resolve issues, that 90% of the value here is real property, that Dexter Chan will never be satisfied, that objections have been filed against the declarations by attorney and that if Ms. Calhoun would ask her questions they will answer them. Mr. Freedman stated that everyone agreed to return to mediation pending Mr. Chan's review of the documents, that they tried to continue this hearing but had to spend time drafting oppositions which are now filed, that an evidentiary hearing is needed on what has been filed and that the case needs to be reset for status but they need to return to mediation first. Mr. Bresler reported that there was no time to respond to the last set of objections to the accounting and the ongoing discovery issue between the parties and that 14 bankers boxes of filed were delivered but they are not available on PDF for everyone to view yet. Ms. Calhoun stated that the parties have different understandings, that the client is entitled to know what happened to the cash assets, that there is no transparency and that the court has the equitable jurisdiction to suspend powers. The Court stated that it is not granting any of that relief today, that at a minimum the matter needs to be continued and the parties need to talk to each other. Ms. Calhoun requested a 60 day continuance for time to ask questions that have arisen from the received documents and requests that the accounting go forward on 8/4/15. Mr. Koplowitz stated that they should resume mediation and asked Ms. Calhoun that in an effort to limit expenses if she is not ready to go forward on 8/4/15 that she continue the case. The Court encouraged the parties to get back to mediation and talk constructively. The Petition for Instructions, The Petition to set aside and cancel the grant deed transferring trust property, etc., and the Petition for Order suspending trustees powers and appoint temporary trustee, etc., were continued to November 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m. in dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Mr. Bresler stated that the principal business and asset of the Trust is here in San Francisco, that the Trustee does most of the work here and spends most of her time as Trustee working on the Trust here. Mr. Freedman stated that the books and records are being maintained at Mr. Bresler's office. Mr. Weaver stated that he received a notice indicating that the principal address was in Millbrae. Mr. Bresler stated that the Trustee believes the percentage of ownership of the property is fifty fifty. Mr. Freedman stated that his Clients thought they were one hundred percent owners, that he did considerable research regarding title, that the petition was brought to return Bessie's third to the Trust and that from their point of view his Client will not be filing an amendment to their petition. Mr. Freedman also stated that there is a pending issue of what happened to money that was borrowed on the property, that his Client has not agreed to dismiss the petition. Mr. Bresler stated that he filed a partition action and that he asked for appointment of a referee to determine ownership on both Trust properties (later clarified that he will be filing said petition). Mr. Weaver stated that the line of credit has been paid down, that his Client asked Bank of the West to reconstruct the grant deed and the only issue is the Ortega property ownership percentages. The Parties discussed the possibility of doing private mediation. Mr. Bresler spoke of the condition of the property (paint chipping/water leaks/mold). The Petition to Set Aside and Cancel Grant Deed, etc. was continued to April 1, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the Parties have arrived at resolution or have a plan to get there.
